source.title: Fire in Pressurized Welding Enclosure - Holstein Platform

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Green Canyon Block 645

actions_taken: Emergency Response Team extinguished fire using nearby hose reel
Zone 4 deluge shut down at approximately 1809 hours
event.summary: On June 27, 2022, at approximately 1755 hours, a fire occurred inside a Pressurized Welding Enclosure (PWE) on the Northeast side of Green Canyon Block 645 Platform A (Holstein). The fire was caused by improperly wrapped Panther felt on Zone 4 deluge wiring, which allowed slag from hot work to smolder inside the wrapping. The fire was discovered when the firewater pump activated and deluge Zone 4 was triggered by the burning wires that controlled the deluge system. Contributing factors included only one Firewatch being present during the 30-minute cool-down period instead of the required two, and fire and smoke detection systems being disabled when the hot work control panel was turned off.
recommendations: Add a second Firewatch to PWE hot work operations to remain on station for the full 30 minutes after hot work is complete
Add the ability of the control room to monitor the status of at least one fire and one gas detection at all times
Separate power supply for fire and smoke detection so that monitoring can continue even after hot work has concluded

hazards.name: Electrical wiring in hot work area

threats.name: Slag from hot work contacting improperly wrapped wiring insulation
Smoldering of felt and wire insulation after hot work completion
Absence of Firewatch during cool-down period
Fire and smoke detection systems disabled during hot work

consequences.name: Fire ignition inside Pressurized Welding Enclosure

controls.name: Firewatch during hot work operations
controls.barrier_role: Detect and suppress fire during hot work
controls.lod_basis: Direct observation and immediate response capability

supporting_text: Safe Zone's (owner of PWE) standard operating procedures is to have two firewatchers during hot work operations, one inside and one outside. However, only one Firewatch was on location at the PWE for the 30 minutes after hot work was completed.
At approximately 1730 hours, the Firewatch stood down.

controls.name: Proper wrapping of Panther felt for hot work protection
controls.barrier_role: Prevent slag from contacting wiring insulation
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier to protect wiring from hot work debris

supporting_text: The BSEE investigation team found that facility wiring inside the PWE had been wrapped incorrectly with Panther felt enabling slag from hot work to get down inside of the wrapping and smolder, causing the wire and felt to catch fire.
The felt was wrapped in such a manner that the tape joint was facing toward the work being conducted.

controls.name: Fire and smoke detection systems in PWE

supporting_text: The BSEE investigation team also noted that fire and smoke detection systems inside of the PWE are part of the power system that enables the hot work to be conducted. When this control panel was turned off, fire and smoke detection systems inside the PWE are also disabled.
Personnel on board were not aware of the fire until approximately 1755 hours when the firewater pump started and deluge Zone 4 activated.
At approximately 1755 hours, the firewater pump kicked on and deluge in Zone 4 activated.
The cause of the activation was not due to fire being detected but because the wire(s) that the fire burned through inside the PWE were lines that control the activation of deluge Zone 4.

controls.name: Emergency Response Team with fire suppression equipment
controls.barrier_role: Manual fire suppression and emergency response
controls.lod_basis: Trained personnel with immediate access to fire suppression equipment

supporting_text: Two members of the Emergency Response Team (ERT) arrived on location at approximately 1758 hours. ERT was able to put the fire out utilizing a nearby hose reel.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
